title: Trisetum evolutum
intro: Trisetum evolutum, also known as the spelt-like grass or evolute oat grass, is a type of grass native to the mountainous regions of the Old World. This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, offering potential health benefits such as antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects, which may help alleviate symptoms of various diseases. The bioactive constituents of Trisetum evolutum include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and other phytochemicals, which contribute to its medicinal properties. Trisetum evolutum is often used in traditional herbal medicine and is prepared in various forms, including teas, infusions, and dried extracts, which are commonly used to support immune function and overall well-being.
taxonomy: Trisetum evolutum belongs to the family Poaceae, commonly known as grasses. It is classified under the tribe Poeae, which includes a variety of grass species. The genus Trisetum is a group of grasses typically found in alpine and subalpine regions. Trisetum evolutum is a specific species within this genus. Its classification is also supported by its unique morphology and characteristics.
